response: The business-research problem is whether Benders decomposition can make a large offline capacitated facility-location model computationally manageable without changing its business decisions or objective. Given candidate sites, demands, costs, and capacities, the model chooses facility openings and commodity flows subject to facility capacity, demand satisfaction, and flow balance, minimizing facility and shipment cost. The method separates binary location choices in a master problem from continuous flow subproblems; cuts from those subproblems progressively refine the master lower bound. Its potential planning value is the ability to solve the same model, or obtain stronger bounds and incumbents, at relevant scale. It does not introduce adaptive operating decisions or real-time control. The claim that the method scales to larger instances remains an inference and requires computational evidence across increasing sizes, with runtime, memory, gaps, and solution quality compared under common conditions. The relevant offline planning scale and any practical time limit are also not supplied.
